description_long: This object is a vintage computer terminal featuring a bulky, rectangular CRT monitor with a slightly curved glass screen displaying green monochrome text. The monitor is encased in a beige plastic housing with black ventilation grilles on the top and sides, and it is paired with a matching full-sized keyboard connected by a coiled cable. The front of the monitor is deeply recessed, creating a shadowed bezel around the display area, and the keyboard has a classic layout with dark keys and a beige frame. The rear and sides of the monitor are plain, with minimal detailing except for ventilation slots and a single round port. The overall design is utilitarian, with sharp edges and a robust, industrial appearance typical of late 1970s or early 1980s computing equipment. No visible branding or decorative elements are present, emphasizing function over form.
description: A vintage beige CRT computer terminal with a recessed screen, black ventilation grilles, and a matching keyboard connected by a coiled cable. The design is boxy and utilitarian, with no visible branding.
description_view_0: Front view showing the curved CRT screen with green text, deep black bezel, and the full keyboard in front.
description_view_1: Right side view highlighting the monitor's depth, top and side ventilation grilles, and the coiled keyboard cable.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the plain back panel with two vertical slots, a round port, and additional ventilation.
description_view_3: Left side view showing the solid side panel, top ventilation, and the keyboard positioned to the right.
attribute_screen_bezel_design: The bezel is thick, deep, and made of matte black plastic, creating a pronounced shadow box effect around the screen with sharp, squared corners and no decorative elements.
attribute_stand_type: The monitor rests directly on a flat, integrated base that is part of the main housing, with no visible adjustability or separate stand components.
attribute_button_placement: No physical buttons or controls are visible on the front, sides, or top of the monitor in the provided views.
